topic: A technician performing a 'Three-Point Bend' test on a carbon fiber sample.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: Front view: technician places carbon fiber sample into the three-point bend fixture.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: Side-angle: technician centers the sample and aligns it with the loading nose.
Shot 3 [00:08-00:08]: Close-up: loading nose presses against center, carbon fiber sample bends under load.
parsed_generation.narration: The sequence follows the technician as they position and secure the carbon fiber sample, align it with the loading nose, and apply the central load to induce bending, all within eight seconds.

feedback:
The output largely covers the three-point bend setup with a technician, but the timestamps are irregular and create a gap before the final shot. To better match the gold standard, use contiguous, non-overlapping [00:00-00:04], [00:04-00:08] style segments and ensure the third shot shows bending progression (and ideally fracture). Also heighten visual cues (two supports, fixture, and loading nose) and keep camera angles aligned with a crisp, action-first cinematic tone.
Failed checks: example_alignment
strengths: Clear shot labeling and shot-by-shot progression.
Consistent focus on the technician and carbon fiber sample across shots.
fixes: Align timestamps to a continuous sequence with non-zero durations (e.g., [00:00-00:04], [00:04-00:08]).

Explicitly depict the fixture setup (two supports) and the bending progression toward fracture to mirror the gold standard; maintain explicit camera angles (wide, side, close-up) with concise, cinematic wording.
